danger_signals.meaning: Law enforcement opposition; illegal activities result in confrontation

danger_signals.visual_form: Ghouls exist as specific enemy type requiring dedicated hunting missions
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural/biological threat distinct from human or corporate opposition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player confined to single area with visible boundaries; other areas blocked or inaccessible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Joshua begins with limited options; cannot traverse city freely

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Taxi system becomes available; visual access to multiple city districts and locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains freedom to choose mission locations and explore multiple factions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visa checkpoints; helicopter bypass routes visible as alternative access methods
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Political boundaries enforced through location access mechanics; negotiation stat influences entry

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Real-time combat display; enemy density and type varies by mission type
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shadowrunners engaged in active combat; AI-controlled allies fight alongside player

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shadowrunner sprites available for hire; visual availability of past runners
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pricing and attitude shift based on mission outcome; successful contracts result in cheaper rehire rates

environmental_storytelling.example: Megacorp headquarters represents corporate power and illegal infiltration work; LoneStar building represents law enforcement opposition and restricted zones
Shadowrun types (gang raids, search & retrieval, extractions, courier, ghoul hunting, Matrix runs) indicate competing interests and multiple threat types in city
Mr. Johnsons distributed across multiple clubs/bars signals decentralized black market job network; shadowrunners scattered throughout city indicates thriving mercenary culture
Shops offering guns, cyberware, spells, and cyberdecks communicate technological stratification and multiple power systems in Shadowrun world
Area access restrictions and helicopter bypass options visually communicate political borders and class-based movement restrictions

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Starting archetype (samurai/decker/shaman) determines initial combat focus and equipment loadout
Player-directed stat customization allows hybrid builds; cyberware reduces magic effectiveness if overused
Enemy type and difficulty varies by mission location (gangs in hangouts, corporate security in megacorp facilities, ghouls in dedicated hunting zones)
Shadowrunner attitude neutral to player; pricing at standard rate
Shadowrunner returns to location; available for cheaper rehire, indicating positive reputation
Shadowrunner upset and charges increased rates; visual unavailability until player reputation recovers
Job pay and difficulty influenced by player's negotiation stat; different Johnsons offer different mission types

notes: Shadowrun Genesis uses environmental design (location types, faction headquarters, shop distribution) and NPC behavior systems to communicate world-state. Political situation emerges through visa/checkpoint mechanics and Mr. Johnson distribution across clubs suggesting decentralized power structure. Danger is signaled through enemy type variety tied to specific locations, establishing gang, corporate, law enforcement, and supernatural threat hierarchies. Faction identity is implicit in location control rather than explicit visual markers—corporate missions center on megacorp aesthetics, gang missions on gang hangouts, law enforcement opposition through LoneStar presence. The Karma system and shadowrunner hiring/pricing mechanics create behavioral tells for political standing and world-state progression.
